file.content:
# Spec: `next` Command — Restrict to `todo` Status Only

## Summary

Change the `next <user-name>` subcommand to only consider issues with `todo` status, removing `idea` and `in-progress` from the eligible statuses. The `next` command is meant to recommend the best piece of work to pick up next — `idea` issues haven't been vetted yet, and `in-progress` issues are already being worked on. Only `todo` represents work that's been triaged, approved, and ready to start.

## Requirements

1. The `Tracker.next(assignee)` method must filter the open index to issues where `status === "todo"` (previously: `status in ["idea", "todo", "in-progress"]`)
2. All other behavior remains unchanged: assignee filter, blockage exclusion, priority/impact/id sorting, `ComputedIssue` return shape, `NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE` when empty
3. Existing tests that rely on the old status filter must be updated to reflect the new behavior
4. New tests should explicitly verify that `idea` and `in-progress` issues are excluded

## API/interface changes

No API changes — same method signature, same return type (`NextResult`). Only the internal status filter changes.

### Changed files

1. **`src/core/tracker.ts`** — In the `next()` method, change the status filter from `["idea", "todo", "in-progress"]` to `["todo"]`
2. **Tests** — Update existing `next` tests and add explicit exclusion tests for `idea` and `in-progress`

## Implementation notes

- This is a single-line change in the filter logic within `Tracker.next()`
- The constant or inline array that defines eligible statuses should be updated
- Search for the existing filter (likely something like `["idea", "todo", "in-progress"].includes(status)`) and narrow to `status === "todo"`
- Update the `NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE` message to say "No todo issues found" instead of "No unblocked issues found" for clarity

## Out of scope

- No changes to the `list` command (it has its own `--status` filter)
- No changes to any other command
- No new CLI flags or options

## Status: REVIEWED

### Decision

Skip review phase — trivially scoped change (single filter line). Reviews add no value here. Go straight to implementation + validation.

# CTO Expertise Index
# Agent: cto
# Domain: Project architecture, technical specifications, development alignment

hierarchy:
  manager: none — top-level agent
  subordinates:
    active:
      - library-developer (implementation)
      - library-quality (testing, documentation, quality gates)

expertise_status: "Dogfooding active. Cap upward auto-promotion complete (mp9xefucmo, done). Active work: restrict `next` command to todo-only status filter (parent mp9zd202cl, in-progress). Reviews skipped — trivially scoped single-line change. Implementation mp9zda04cp → library-developer (unblocked). Validation mp9zda5d9n → library-quality (blocked by implementation). Pending issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9)."

project_overview:
  name: trackgentic
  type: npm library (TypeScript, Bun runtime)
  purpose: Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly
  distribution: npm package with CLI entry point (`trackgentic` command)
  stage: "Dogfooding active. Cap upward auto-promotion complete (mp9xefucmo). Agent runner live. Current work: restrict `next` to todo-only (parent mp9zd202cl, in-progress). Standing issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9)."
  bun_version: "1.3.11"
  build_tool: tsup (implemented, producing dist/)
  docs_tool: TypeDoc HTML output (configured, generates to docs-html/)

detail_files:
  - path: architecture.yaml
    description: Core data model, file structure, event sourcing, resolution algorithm, index design
  - path: design-decisions.yaml
    description: Architectural decisions and rationale (why file-backed, why event-sourced, etc.)
  - path: status.yaml
    description: Current project status, implementation roadmap, open questions, risks

specifications:
  location: .agentic/specs/
  files:
    - path: 01-overview.md
      description: Monorepo structure, library package layout, design principles, dependencies, CLI/API relationship
    - path: 02-data-model.md
      description: All TypeScript types, interfaces, file formats, initial file contents
    - path: 03-api-specification.md
      description: Every Tracker method with params, return types, behavior, error handling
    - path: 04-cli-specification.md
      description: Every CLI command with args, flags, auth, output format, error handling
    - path: 05-implementation-phases.md
      description: 7 phases from foundation to polish, with tasks, tests, and exit criteria per phase
    - path: 06-quality-plan.md
      description: Quality plan — now uses Prettier + ESLint (was Biome). Linting, typechecking (strict+), testing (Bun), test organization, documentation (TypeDoc), quality gates
    - path: 07-phase3-auth-users.md
      description: Phase 3 specification — auth module (resolveAuthor), user CRUD (register/list/revoke/regenerate), auth integration into existing methods, CLI user commands
    - path: 07-phase4-comments-spec.md
      description: Phase 4 specification — computeComments function, 4 Tracker comment methods, CLI comment commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase5-hierarchy-spec.md
      description: Phase 5 specification — childrenOf map management, hierarchy.ts (upward/downward constraints), Tracker create/update integration, reparenting, test plan
    - path: 07-phase6-blockages-spec.md
      description: Phase 6 specification — dependency-manager (bidirectional maps, cycle detection, batch atomicity, impact score), 4 Tracker blockage methods, auto-resolution on status change, CLI blockage commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase7-polish-spec.md
      description: Phase 7 specification — JSDoc on all public API, error message polish, edge case & resilience testing, package configuration verification
    - path: next-command-spec.md
      description: "`next <user-name>` command — recommend highest-priority unblocked issue for a user. Returns ComputedIssue or NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE. Filters by assignee, actionable statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), no active blockages. Sort: priority ASC → impact DESC → id ASC."
    - path: cap-upward-promotion-spec.md
      description: "Cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress. Parents never auto-promote to done/closed via computeUpwardPromotions. Spec status: REVIEWED. Reason format: auto-promoted to TARGET: child advanced to CHILD. 7 new test cases required."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)

    summary: "Read all docs, built full understanding of architecture. Could not write expertise files (missing Write/Edit tools). Requested tool update."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)
